applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile
applied_optimizations.description: Used torch.compile(mode='default') to optimize the model. This is the recommended first optimization according to the amd-kernel-optimization skill doc.

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton fused GELU+GroupNorm kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Numerical precision issues with large tensors (128x64x258x258). The naive variance accumulation in fp32 caused max diff ~0.001-0.002, exceeding the 1e-4 tolerance.

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton GroupNorm with two-pass variance
attempted_but_failed.reason: Even with two-pass variance computation, accumulated floating point errors over ~532K elements per group caused max diff ~0.00019, still above 1e-4 tolerance.

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton fused kernel with float64 accumulators
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton type consistency issues - could not properly maintain float64 type for accumulators while storing float32 output.
attempted_but_failed.error: CompilationError: Loop-carried variable has inconsistent type

notes: torch.compile(mode='default') provides 1.17x speedup with exact numerical correctness
Triton kernel approach faced numerical precision challenges with large tensors
The reference model does NOT pass 'groups' parameter to ConvTranspose2d despite accepting it in __init__
PyTorch's default GELU uses exact CDF formula, not tanh approximation
